title: Discounted Cash Flow: A project is expected to have cash inflows of 30000...
summary: Apply DCF analysis to determine the present value of projected future cash flows.
prompt: A project is expected to have cash inflows of $30,000 in Year 1, $40,000 in Year 2, and $50,000 in Year 3. If the required rate of return is 12%, what is the NPV of this project?
solution: Step 1: Identify cash flows and the required return. Cash inflows: Year 1 = $30,000, Year 2 = $40,000, Year 3 = $50,000; r = 12%. Step 2: Calculate NPV using the formula: NPV = Σ (CF / (1 + r)^t). Step 3: Calculate NPV: NPV = ($30,000 / 1.12) + ($40,000 / (1.12)^2) + ($50,000 / (1.12)^3) = $26,785.71 + $31,847.36 + $35,697.23 = $94,330.30.
